module pe (
    input clk,
    input rst,
    input [31:0] a,
    input [31:0] b,
    output reg [31:0] c
);

always @(posedge clk or posedge rst) begin
    
    if(rst) begin
        
        c <= 32'b0;
    end else begin
        
        c <= c + (a * b);
    end
end
endmodule

samples.xml_description:
<module id="multi_8bit">
    <description>Multiplies two 8-bit operands using shift-and-add method.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="A" width_description="[7:0]">First 8-bit input operand (multiplicand).</input>
        <input id="B" width_description="[7:0]">Second 8-bit input operand (multiplier).</input>
        <output id="product" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit output representing the product of the two 8-bit inputs.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        // Initialize variables
        integer i;
        reg [15:0] temp_product = 0;
        reg [7:0] multiplicand = A;
        
        // Perform multiplication using shift-and-add method
        for (i = 0; i < 8; i = i + 1) begin
            if (B[i]) begin
                temp_product = temp_product + (multiplicand << i);
            end
        end
        
        // Assign final product to output port
        assign product = temp_product;
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="parallel2serial">
    <description>Parallel to serial converter, converts every 4 bits of parallel input to 1 bit of serial output (MSB to LSB).</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operations.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="d" width_description="[3:0]">4-bit parallel data input.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Valid signal indicating the availability of serial output.</output>
        <output id="dout">Serial output representing the converted data.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>Assigns the most significant bit of the parallel input to the serial output (dout). On each clock cycle, if the counter (cnt) is 3, indicating the last bit of the parallel input, the module updates the data register (data) with the parallel input (d), resets the counter (cnt) to 0, and sets the valid signal (valid) to 1. Otherwise, the module increments the counter (cnt) by 1, sets the valid signal (valid) to 0, and shifts the data register (data) one bit to the left, with the most significant bit shifted to the least significant bit. Counter Register: If the reset signal (rst_n) is high, the register (cnt) is incremented by 1.</implementation>
</module>

module width_8to16 (
    input 				   clk,
    input 				   rst_n,
    input				      valid_in,
    input	   [7:0]		   data_in,
    output	reg			valid_out,
    output   reg [15:0]	data_out
);

reg [7:0] data_lock;
reg flag;

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    if (!rst_n) begin
        data_out <= 0;
        valid_out <= 0;
    end
    else begin
        if (flag && valid_in) begin
            data_out <= {data_lock, data_in};
            valid_out <= 1;
        end
        else begin
            valid_out <= 0;
        end
    end
end

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    if (!rst_n) begin
        flag <= 0;
    end
    else begin
        if (valid_in) begin
            flag <= ~flag;
        end
    end
end

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    if (!rst_n) begin
        data_lock <= 0;
    end
    else begin
        if (valid_in && !flag) begin
            data_lock <= data_in;
        end
    end
end

endmodule

<module id="serial2parallel">
    <description>Serial-to-Parallel Conversion Circuit</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="din_serial">Serial input data.</input>
        <input id="din_valid">Validity signal for input data.</input>
        <output id="dout_parallel" width_description="[7:0]">Parallel output data (8 bits wide).</output>
        <output id="dout_valid">Validity signal for the output data.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant id="COUNTER_WIDTH">4</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="cnt" width_description="[COUNTER_WIDTH-1:0]">Counter to keep track of the number of serial input data bits received.</reg>
        <reg id="dout_parallel" width_description="[7:0]">Parallel output data register.</reg>
        <reg id="dout_valid">Valid output signal register.</reg>
        
        // Sequential logic block triggered on the positive edge of clk or negative edge of rst_n
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                cnt <= 0;
                dout_parallel <= 0;
                dout_valid <= 0;
            end else begin
                if (din_valid) begin
                    dout_parallel[cnt] <= din_serial;
                    cnt <= cnt + 1;
                    
                    // Check if all 8 serial data bits have been received
                    if (cnt == 7) begin
                        dout_valid <= 1;
                        cnt <= 0;
                    end else begin
                        dout_valid <= 0;
                    end
                end
            end
        end
    </logic_description>
</module>

<module id="edge_detect">
    <description>Edge detector for detecting rising and falling edges of a 1-bit input signal 'a'.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="a">Input signal.</input>
        <output id="rise">Output signal indicating a rising edge.</output>
        <output id="down">Output signal indicating a falling edge.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        // Sequential logic block triggered on the positive edge of the clock or negative edge of reset
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                // On reset, initialize the previous value of 'a' to 0
                prev_a <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                // Update the previous value of 'a' with the current value of 'a'
                prev_a <= a;
            end
        end
        
        // Combinational logic to detect rising and falling edges
        assign rise = (prev_a == 1'b0 && a == 1'b1);
        assign down = (prev_a == 1'b1 && a == 1'b0);
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="div_8bit">
    <description>A simplified radix-2 divider for 8-bit signed or unsigned integers.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">The clock signal for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst">The reset signal to initialize or reset the module.</input>
        <input id="sign">Indicates if the operation is signed (1) or unsigned (0).</input>
        <input id="dividend" width_description="[7:0]">The 8-bit input signal representing the dividend for division.</input>
        <input id="divisor" width_description="[7:0]">The 8-bit input signal representing the divisor for division.</input>
        <input id="opn_valid">A 1-bit input signal indicating that a valid operation request is present.</input>
        <input id="res_ready">A 1-bit input signal indicating that the 16-bit output result has already been acknowledged.</input>
        <output id="res_valid">A 1-bit output signal indicating the result is valid and ready.</output>
        <output id="result" width_description="[15:0]">The 16-bit output containing the remainder in the upper 8 bits and the quotient in the lower 8 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="IDLE">Idle state.</state>
        <state id="DIVIDE">Divide state.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="res_valid_reg" type="DFF" init_value="0"/>
        <reg id="start_cnt" type="DFF" init_value="0"/>
        <reg id="cnt" type="DFF" init_value="0"/>
        <reg id="neg_divisor" type="DFF" init_value="0"/>
        <reg id="dividend_sign" type="DFF" init_value="0"/>
        <reg id="divisor_sign" type="DFF" init_value="0"/>
        <reg id="dividend_abs" type="DFF" init_value="0"/>
        <reg id="divisor_abs" type="DFF" init_value="0"/>
        <reg id="result_reg" type="DFF" init_value="0"/>
        <wire id="sub_result" width_description="[9:0]"/>
        <wire id="carry_out"/>
        <wire id="mux_out"/>
        <assign id="sub_result" value="{SR[8], SR} + {NEG_DIVISOR[8], NEG_DIVISOR}"/>
        <assign id="carry_out" value="~sub_result[9]"/>
        <assign id="mux_out" value={sub_result[8:0]} & {9{carry_out}} | {sub_result[8:0]} & ~{9{carry_out}});
        <always_comb>
            case(cnt)
                8'h0: result_reg = {SR[8:0], 1'b0};
                default: result_reg = {mux_out, SR[8:1]};
            endcase
        end
        <always @(posedge clk or posedge rst)> 
            if(rst) begin
                res_valid_reg <= 1'b0;
                start_cnt <= 1'b0;
                cnt <= 1'b0;
                neg_divisor <= 1'b0;
                dividend_sign <= 1'b0;
                divisor_sign <= 1'b0;
                dividend_abs <= 1'b0;
                divisor_abs <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                if(opn_valid && !res_valid_reg) begin
                    dividend_sign <= sign ? dividend[7] : 1'b0;
                    divisor_sign <= sign ? divisor[7] : 1'b0;
                    dividend_abs <= sign ? (dividend[7] ? (~dividend + 1'b1) : dividend) : dividend;
                    divisor_abs <= sign ? (divisor[7] ? (~divisor + 1'b1) : divisor) : divisor;
                    SR <= {dividend_abs, 1'b0};
                    NEG_DIVISOR <= ~(divisor_abs) + 1'b1;
                    cnt <= 1'b1;
                    start_cnt <= 1'b1;
                end else if(start_cnt) begin
                    if(cnt[7]) begin
                        cnt <= 1'b0;
                        start_cnt <= 1'b0;
                        SR <= result_reg;
                    end else begin
                        cnt <= cnt + 1'b1;
                        SR <= {mux_out, SR[8:1]};
                    end
                end
            end
        end
        <always @(posedge clk or posedge rst)) begin
            if(rst) begin
                res_valid_reg <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                if(cnt == 8) begin
                    res_valid_reg <= 1'b1;
                end else if(res_valid_reg && res_ready) begin
                    res_valid_reg <= 1'b0;
                end else begin
                    res_valid_reg <= res_valid_reg;
                end
            end
        end
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        When opn_valid is high and res_valid is low, the module saves the inputs dividend and divisor.
        Initializes the shift register SR with the absolute value of the dividend shifted left by one bit.
        Sets NEG_DIVISOR to the negated absolute value of the divisor.
        Sets the counter cnt to 1 and start_cnt to 1 to begin the division process.
        Division Process(If start_cnt is high, the module performs the following steps):
        If the counter cnt reaches 8 (most significant bit of cnt is set), the division is complete:
        cnt and start_cnt are cleared.
        Updates the shift register SR with the final remainder and quotient.
        Otherwise, the counter cnt is incremented, and the shift register SR is updated based on the subtraction result:
        Computes the subtraction of NEG_DIVISOR.
        Uses a multiplexer to select the appropriate result based on the carry-out.
        Updates SR by shifting left and inserting the carry-out.
        Result Validity:
        res_valid is managed based on the reset signal, the counter, and whether the result has been consumed.
        res_valid should be update directly, without passing middle register value.
        res_valid is set to zero if rst is high.
        Otherwise, if cnt reaches 8, res_ready is set to 1.
        Otherwise, if res_valid and res_ready is all high, res_valid is set to 0.
        Otherwise, res_valid is set to res_valid.
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="traffic_light">
    <description>A traffic light system with red, yellow, and green indicators, and a pedestrian button.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="pass_request">Request signal for pedestrians to cross.</input>
        <output id="clock" width_description="[7:0]">Count value of the internal counter.</output>
        <output id="red">Red signal indicating the status of the traffic light.</output>
        <output id="yellow">Yellow signal indicating the status of the traffic light.</output>
        <output id="green">Green signal indicating the status of the traffic light.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state_value id="idle">State value for idle state.</state_value>
        <state_value id="s1_red">State value for red state.</state_value>
        <state_value id="s2_yellow">State value for yellow state.</state_value>
        <state_value id="s3_green">State value for green state.</state_value>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <register id="cnt" width_description="[7:0]">Internal counter for timing control.</register>
        <register id="state" width_description="[1:0]">Current state of the traffic light controller.</register>
        <register id="p_red">Previous value of the red signal.</register>
        <register id="p_yellow">Previous value of the yellow signal.</register>
        <register id="p_green">Previous value of the green signal.</register>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        // State transition logic
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                state <= idle;
                p_red <= 0;
                p_yellow <= 0;
                p_green <= 0;
            end else begin
                case(state)
                    idle: begin
                        p_red <= 0;
                        p_yellow <= 0;
                        p_green <= 0;
                        state <= s1_red;
                    end
                    s1_red: begin
                        p_red <= 1;
                        p_yellow <= 0;
                        p_green <= 0;
                        if(cnt == 3) begin
                            state <= s3_green;
                        end else begin
                            state <= s1_red;
                        end
                    end
                    s2_yellow: begin
                        p_red <= 0;
                        p_yellow <= 1;
                        p_green <= 0;
                        if(cnt == 3) begin
                            state <= s1_red;
                        end else begin
                            state <= s2_yellow;
                        end
                    end
                    s3_green: begin
                        p_red <= 0;
                        p_yellow <= 0;
                        p_green <= 1;
                        if(cnt == 3) begin
                            state <= s2_yellow;
                        end else begin
                            state <= s3_green;
                        end
                    end
                endcase
            end
        end
        
        // Counting logic
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if(!rst_n) begin
                cnt <= 10;
            end else begin
                if(pass_request && green) begin
                    cnt <= 10;
                end else if(!green && p_green) begin
                    cnt <= 60;
                end else if(!yellow && p_yellow) begin
                    cnt <= 5;
                end else if(!red && p_red) begin
                    cnt <= 10;
                end else begin
                    cnt <= cnt - 1;
                end
            end
        end
        
        // Assign statements for outputs
        assign red = p_red;
        assign yellow = p_yellow;
        assign green = p_green;
        
        // Assign statement for clock output
        assign clock = cnt;
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="multi_16bit">
    <description>Implement an unsigned 16-bit multiplier using shift and accumulate operations.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Chip clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for chip reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="start">Chip enable signal to initiate the multiplication operation.</input>
        <input id="ain" width_description="[15:0]">Input signal representing the multiplicand (a) with a data width of 16 bits.</input>
        <input id="bin" width_description="[15:0]">Input signal representing the multiplier (b) with a data width of 16 bits.</input>
        <output id="yout" width_description="[31:0]">Product output signal with a data width of 32 bits.</output>
        <output id="done">Chip output flag signal. Defined as 1 indicates the completion of the multiplication operation.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant id="IDLE">0</constant>
        <constant id="SHIFT_ACCUMULATE">1</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <signal id="i" type="reg" width_description="[4:0]">Shift count register initialized to 0.</signal>
        <signal id="done_r" type="reg">Multiplication completion flag initialized to 0.</signal>
        <signal id="areg" type="reg" width_description="[15:0]">Register to store the multiplicand (a).</signal>
        <signal id="breg" type="reg" width_description="[15:0]">Register to store the multiplier (b).</signal>
        <signal id="yout_r" type="reg" width_description="[31:0]">Register to store the product output (yout).</signal>
        
        // Sequential logic block triggered on the rising edge of the clock or the negative edge of the reset signal
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (~rst_n) begin
                // Reset the shift count register to 0
                i <= 0;
            end else if (start && i < 17) begin
                // Increment the shift count register by 1
                i <= i + 1;
            end else begin
                // Reset the shift count register to 0
                i <= 0;
            end
        end
        
        // Sequential logic block triggered on the rising edge of the clock or the negative edge of the reset signal
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (~rst_n) begin
                // Set the multiplication completion flag to 0
                done_r <= 0;
            end else if (i == 16) begin
                // Set the multiplication completion flag to 1
                done_r <= 1;
            end else if (i == 17) begin
                // Reset the multiplication completion flag to 0
                done_r <= 0;
            end
        end
        
        // Sequential logic block triggered on the rising edge of the clock or the negative edge of the reset signal
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (~rst_n) begin
                // Initialize the multiplicand register to 0
                areg <= 0;
                // Initialize the multiplier register to 0
                breg <= 0;
                // Initialize the product output register to 0
                yout_r <= 0;
            end else if (start) begin
                // Perform shift and accumulate operation
                case(i)
                    0: begin
                        // Store the multiplicand (a) in the multiplicand register
                        areg <= ain;
                        // Store the multiplier (b) in the multiplier register
                        breg <= bin;
                    end
                    default: begin
                        // Accumulate the product output based on the current bit of the multiplicand register
                        if(areg[i-1]) begin
                            // Shift the multiplier register by i-1 positions and append zeros at the least significant bit positions
                            yout_r <= yout_r + {breg[15:0], {(i-1){1'b0}}};
                        end
                    end
                endcase
            end
        end
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>Sequential logic block triggered on the rising edge of the clock or the negative edge of the reset signal. If the reset signal is low, initialize the shift count register to 0. Otherwise, increment the shift count register by 1 if the start signal is active and the shift count register is less than 17. Sequential logic block triggered on the rising edge of the clock or the negative edge of the reset signal. If the reset signal is low, set the multiplication completion flag to 0. Otherwise, set the multiplication completion flag to 1 if the shift count register is equal to 16, and reset the multiplication completion flag to 0 if the shift count register is equal to 17. Sequential logic block triggered on the rising edge of the clock or the negative edge of the reset signal. If the reset signal is low, initialize the multiplicand register to 0, the multiplier register to 0, and the product output register to 0. Otherwise, perform shift and accumulate operation based on the current bit of the multiplicand register. If the start signal is active, store the multiplicand (a) in the multiplicand register and the multiplier (b) in the multiplier register. For shift counts greater than 0 and less than 17, if the bit at position i-1 in the multiplicand register is high, accumulate the product output by shifting the multiplier register by i-1 positions and appending zeros at the least significant bit positions.</implementation>
    <task>
        <id>reset</id>
        <description>Reset the shift count register to 0.</description>
    </task>
    <task>
        <id>increment_shift_count</id>
        <description>Increment the shift count register by 1 if the start signal is active and the shift count register is less than 17.</description>
    </task>
    <task>
        <id>set_done_flag</id>
        <description>Set the multiplication completion flag to 1 if the shift count register is equal to 16, and reset the multiplication completion flag to 0 if the shift count register is equal to 17.</description>
    </task>
    <task>
        <id>shift_accumulate_operation</id>
        <description>Perform shift and accumulate operation based on the current bit of the multiplicand register. If the start signal is active, store the multiplicand (a) in the multiplicand register and the multiplier (b) in the multiplier register. For shift counts greater than 0 and less than 17, if the bit at position i-1 in the multiplicand register is high, accumulate the product output by shifting the multiplier register by i-1 positions and appending zeros at the least significant bit positions.</description>
    </task>
</module>

<module id="signal_generator">
    <description>Triangle Wave Signal Generator</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <output id="wave" width_description="[4:0]">Generated waveform signal.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="0">Initial state.</state>
        <state id="1">Incrementing state.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="state" width_description="[1:0]">Register to store the current state.</reg>
        <reg id="wave" width_description="[4:0]">Register to store the generated waveform.</reg>
        
        // Sequential logic block triggered on positive edge of clk or negative edge of rst_n
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (~rst_n) begin
                // Reset condition: set state to 0 and wave to 0
                state <= 0;
                wave <= 0;
            end else begin
                // State machine to generate triangle wave pattern
                case(state)
                    0: begin
                        // Increment wave value by 1
                        wave <= wave + 1;
                        
                        // Check if wave has reached maximum value (31)
                        if(wave == 31) begin
                            // Transition to next state (incrementing state)
                            state <= 1;
                        end
                    end
                    
                    1: begin
                        // Decrement wave value by 1
                        wave <= wave - 1;
                        
                        // Check if wave has reached minimum value (0)
                        if(wave == 0) begin
                            // Transition back to initial state
                            state <= 0;
                        end
                    end
                endcase
            end
        end
    </logic_description>
    
    <implementation>Generates a triangular waveform by incrementing and decrementing the wave signal.</implementation>
</module>

module calendar (
    input CLK,
    input RST,
    output reg [5:0] Hours,
    output reg [5:0] Mins,
    output reg [5:0] Secs
);

always @(posedge CLK or posedge RST) begin
    if (RST) begin
        Secs <= 0;
    end else begin
        if (Secs == 59) begin
            Secs <= 0;
        end else begin
            Secs <= Secs + 1;
        end
    end
end

always @(posedge CLK or posedge RST) begin
    if (RST) begin
        Mins <= 0;
    end else begin
        if (Mins == 59 && Secs == 59) begin
            Mins <= 0;
        end else if (Secs == 59) begin
            Mins <= Mins + 1;
        end
    end
end

always @(posedge CLK or posedge RST) begin
    if (RST) begin
        Hours <= 0;
    end else begin
        if (Hours == 23 && Mins == 59 && Secs == 59) begin
            Hours <= 0;
        end else if (Mins == 59 && Secs == 59) begin
            Hours <= Hours + 1;
        end
    end
end

endmodule

module JC_counter (
    input                clk,
    input                rst_n,
    output reg [63:0]     Q
);

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    if (~rst_n) begin
        
        Q <= 64'b0;
    end else begin
        
        if (Q[0] == 1'b0) begin
            Q <= {1'b1, Q[63:1]};
        end else begin
            Q <= {1'b0, Q[63:1]};
        end
    end
end
endmodule

<module id="JC_counter">
    <description>64-bit Johnson Counter</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal to initialize the counter.</input>
        <output id="Q" width_description="[63:0]">64-bit register representing the current count value.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>Sequential logic block triggered on the positive edge of the clock signal (posedge clk) or the negative edge of the reset signal (negedge rst_n). If the reset signal is active (rst_n == 0), the counter value (Q) is initialized to 0. Otherwise, based on the least significant bit (Q[0]), the counter either increments or decrements its value by shifting the bits to the right and appending a 1 or 0 at the most significant bit position.</implementation>
</module>

<module id="freq_div">
    <description>Frequency Divider for generating multiple clock signals based on input clock frequency.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="CLK_in">Input clock signal.</input>
        <input id="RST">Reset signal.</input>
        <output id="CLK_50">Output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 2.</output>
        <output id="CLK_10">Output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 10.</output>
        <output id="CLK_1">Output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 100.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        // Counter for CLK_50
        reg [0:0] cnt_50;
        always @(posedge CLK_in or posedge RST) begin
            if(RST) begin
                cnt_50 <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                cnt_50 <= ~cnt_50;
            end
        end
        
        // Counter for CLK_10
        reg [3:0] cnt_10;
        always @(posedge CLK_in or posedge RST) begin
            if(RST) begin
                cnt_10 <= 4'd0;
            end else if(cnt_10 == 4'd4) begin
                cnt_10 <= 4'd0;
            end else begin
                cnt_10 <= cnt_10 + 1;
            end
        end
        
        // Counter for CLK_1
        reg [6:0] cnt_100;
        always @(posedge CLK_in or posedge RST) begin
            if(RST) begin
                cnt_100 <= 7'd0;
            end else if(cnt_100 == 7'd49) begin
                cnt_100 <= 7'd0;
            end else begin
                cnt_100 <= cnt_100 + 1;
            end
        end
        
        // Assigning output clocks
        assign CLK_50 = cnt_50[0];
        assign CLK_10 = cnt_10[0];
        assign CLK_1 = cnt_100[0];
        
    </implementation>
</module>

module RAM #(
    parameter WIDTH=6,
    parameter DEPTH=8
) (
    input clk,
    input rst_n,
    input write_en,
    input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0]write_addr,
    input [WIDTH-1:0]write_data,
    input read_en,
    input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0]read_addr,
    output reg [WIDTH-1:0]read_data
);
    reg [WIDTH-1:0] RAM [DEPTH-1:0];
    integer i;
    initial begin
        for(i = 0; i < DEPTH; i = i + 1) begin
            RAM[i] = {WIDTH{1'b0}};
        end
    end
    
    always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
        if(!rst_n) begin
            for(i = 0; i < DEPTH; i = i + 1) begin
                RAM[i] <= {WIDTH{1'b0}};
            end
        end else if(write_en) begin
            RAM[write_addr] <= write_data;
        end
    end
    
    always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
        if(!rst_n) begin
            read_data <= {WIDTH{1'b0}};
        end else if(read_en) begin
            read_data <= RAM[read_addr];
        end else begin
            read_data <= {WIDTH{1'b0}};
        end
    end
endmodule

<module id="RAM">
    <description>A dual-port RAM with a depth of 8 and a bit width of 6, initialized to 000000. Supports simultaneous read and write operations.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="write_en">Write enable signal for initiating write operations.</input>
        <input id="write_addr" width_description="[5:0]">Address for write operations.</input>
        <input id="write_data" width_description="[5:0]">Data to be written to the RAM.</input>
        <input id="read_en">Read enable signal for initiating read operations.</input>
        <input id="read_addr" width_description="[5:0]">Address for read operations.</input>
        <output id="read_data" width_description="[5:0]">Output data read from the RAM.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant id="WIDTH">6</constant>
        <constant id="DEPTH">8</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        <reg id="read_data" width_description="[5:0]">Register to hold the read data.</reg>
        <reg id="RAM" width_description="[DEPTH-1:0][WIDTH-1:0]">Array to store the RAM data.</reg>
        
        // Initialize RAM contents to 000000
        integer i, j;
        initial begin
            for(i = 0; i < DEPTH; i = i + 1) begin
                for(j = 0; j < WIDTH; j = j + 1) begin
                    RAM[i][j] = 1'b0;
                end
            end
        end
        
        // Sequential logic for writing data to RAM
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                for(i = 0; i < DEPTH; i = i + 1) begin
                    for(j = 0; j < WIDTH; j = j + 1) begin
                        RAM[i][j] <= 1'b0;
                    end
                end
            end else if(write_en) begin
                RAM[write_addr] <= write_data;
            end
        end
        
        // Sequential logic for reading data from RAM
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                read_data <= 6'b000000;
            end else if(read_en) begin
                read_data <= RAM[read_addr];
            end else begin
                read_data <= 6'b000000;
            end
        end
    </logic_description>
    
    <implementation>Describes the dual-port RAM functionality, including initialization, writing, and reading operations.</implementation>
</module>

<module id="synchronizer">
    <description>A multi-bit MUX-based synchronizer. Data remains constant during the period when data_en is high, and data_en is high for at least 3 clk_b clock cycles. When the value of data_en is high, data can be synchronized. The data change frequency of data_in is very low. The change interval between two adjacent data is at least 10 clk_b clock cycles.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk_a">Clock signal A used for synchronous operations.</input>
        <input id="clk_b">Clock signal B used for synchronous operations.</input>
        <input id="arstn">Active-low reset signal A. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="brstn">Active-low reset signal B. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[3:0]">Input data signal of size 4 bits.</input>
        <input id="data_en">Input enable signal that controls the selection operation.</input>
        <output id="dataout" width_description="[3:0]">Output data signal of size 4 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        // Data Register
        reg [3:0] data_reg;
        always @(posedge clk_a or negedge arstn) begin
            if (~arstn) begin
                data_reg <= 4'd0;
            end else begin
                data_reg <= data_in;
            end
        end
        
        // Enable Data Register
        reg en_data_reg;
        always @(posedge clk_a or negedge arstn) begin
            if (~arstn) begin
                en_data_reg <= 1'd0;
            end else begin
                en_data_reg <= data_en;
            end
        end
        
        // Enable Control Registers
        reg en_clap_one;
        reg en_clap_two;
        
        always @(posedge clk_b or negedge brstn) begin
            if (~brstn) begin
                en_clap_one <= 1'd0;
                en_clap_two <= 1'd0;
            end else begin
                en_clap_one <= en_data_reg;
                en_clap_two <= en_clap_one;
            end
        end
        
        // Output Assignment
        reg [3:0] dataout;
        always @(posedge clk_b or negedge brstn) begin
            if (~brstn) begin
                dataout <= 4'd0;
            end else begin
                if (en_clap_two) begin
                    dataout <= data_reg;
                end else begin
                    dataout <= dataout;
                end
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="accu">
    <description>Accumulates 4 input data and outputs the result.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock input for synchronization.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input data for addition.</input>
        <input id="valid_in">Input signal indicating readiness for new data.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Output signal indicating when 4 input data accumulation is reached.</output>
        <output id="data_out" width_description="[9:0]">10-bit output data representing the accumulated sum.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        // Internal signals
        reg [9:0] accumulator;
        reg [1:0] counter;
        reg valid_out_reg;
        
        // Sequential logic for accumulator and counter
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                accumulator <= 10'd0;
                counter <= 2'd0;
                valid_out_reg <= 1'b0;
            end else if (valid_in) begin
                if (counter == 2'd3) begin
                    accumulator <= accumulator + {2'b00, data_in};
                    counter <= 2'd0;
                    valid_out_reg <= 1'b1;
                end else begin
                    accumulator <= accumulator + {2'b00, data_in};
                    counter <= counter + 2'd1;
                    valid_out_reg <= 1'b0;
                end
            end
        end
        
        // Assignments for output ports
        assign data_out = accumulator;
        assign valid_out = valid_out_reg;
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="multi_pipe_8bit">
    <description>Design an 8-bit unsigned multiplier using pipelined processing. Implement sequential logic for input control, input registers, partial product generation, partial sum calculation, and final product calculation. Ensure proper synchronization and data flow through the pipeline stages.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal for chip reset.</input>
        <input id="mul_en_in">Input enable signal controlling multiplication operation.</input>
        <input id="mul_a" width_description="[7:0]">Multiplicand input with a data width of 8 bits.</input>
        <input id="mul_b" width_description="[7:0]">Multiplier input with a data width of 8 bits.</input>
        <output id="mul_en_out">Output enable signal indicating if multiplication operation is enabled.</output>
        <output id="mul_out" width_description="[15:0]">Product output with a data width of 16 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="IDLE">State IDLE = 3'b000.</state>
        <state id="LOAD">State LOAD = 3'b001.</state>
        <state id="CALC">State CALC = 3'b010.</state>
        <state id="OUT">State OUT = 3'b011.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="mul_en_out_reg" width_description="[2:0]">Register to hold the output enable signal.</reg>
        <reg id="mul_a_reg" width_description="[7:0]">Register to hold the multiplicand input.</reg>
        <reg id="mul_b_reg" width_description="[7:0]">Register to hold the multiplier input.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[0]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the first partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[1]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the second partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[2]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the third partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[3]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the fourth partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[4]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the fifth partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[5]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the sixth partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[6]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the seventh partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[7]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the eighth partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[0]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the first partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[1]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the second partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[2]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the third partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[3]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the fourth partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[4]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the fifth partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[5]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the sixth partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="mul_out_reg" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the final product output.</reg>
        
        // Sequential logic block triggered on the positive edge of the clock signal
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                // Reset condition: Set all registers to zero
                mul_en_out_reg <= 3'b0;
                mul_a_reg <= 8'b0;
                mul_b_reg <= 8'b0;
                temp[0] <= 16'b0;
                temp[1] <= 16'b0;
                temp[2] <= 16'b0;
                temp[3] <= 16'b0;
                temp[4] <= 16'b0;
                temp[5] <= 16'b0;
                temp[6] <= 16'b0;
                temp[7] <= 16'b0;
                sum[0] <= 16'b0;
                sum[1] <= 16'b0;
                sum[2] <= 16'b0;
                sum[3] <= 16'b0;
                sum[4] <= 16'b0;
                sum[5] <= 16'b0;
                mul_out_reg <= 16'b0;
            end else begin
                // Update the output enable register with the input enable signal
                mul_en_out_reg <= {mul_en_out_reg[1:0], mul_en_in};
                
                // Update the multiplicand register with the input value
                if (mul_en_out_reg[2]) begin
                    mul_a_reg <= mul_a;
                end
                
                // Update the multiplier register with the input value
                if (mul_en_out_reg[2]) begin
                    mul_b_reg <= mul_b;
                end
                
                // Generate partial products based on the multiplier bits
                temp[0] <= {{8{mul_b_reg[0]}}, mul_a_reg} << 0;
                temp[1] <= {{8{mul_b_reg[1]}}, mul_a_reg} << 1;
                temp[2] <= {{8{mul_b_reg[2]}}, mul_a_reg} << 2;
                temp[3] <= {{8{mul_b_reg[3]}}, mul_a_reg} << 3;
                temp[4] <= {{8{mul_b_reg[4]}}, mul_a_reg} << 4;
                temp[5] <= {{8{mul_b_reg[5]}}, mul_a_reg} << 5;
                temp[6] <= {{8{mul_b_reg[6]}}, mul_a_reg} << 6;
                temp[7] <= {{8{mul_b_reg[7]}}, mul_a_reg} << 7;
                
                // Calculate partial sums based on the generated partial products
                sum[0] <= temp[0];
                sum[1] <= temp[1] + temp[2];
                sum[2] <= temp[3] + temp[4];
                sum[3] <= temp[5] + temp[6];
                sum[4] <= temp[7];
                sum[5] <= sum[1] + sum[2];
                sum[6] <= sum[3] + sum[4];
                sum[7] <= sum[5] + sum[6];
                
                // Accumulate the final product
                mul_out_reg <= sum[0] + sum[7];
            end
        end
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>Sequential logic blocks for input control, input registers, partial product generation, partial sum calculation, and final product calculation.</implementation>

<module id="fsm">
    <description>Mealy FSM detection circuit that detects a single-bit input IN. When the input is 10011, output MATCH is 1, and MATCH is 0 in other cases. Support for continuous input and loop detection.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="IN">Input signal to the FSM.</input>
        <input id="CLK">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="RST">Reset signal to initialize the FSM.</input>
        <output id="MATCH">Output signal indicating a match condition based on the FSM state.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="S0">Initial state.</state>
        <state id="S1">State after detecting '1'.</state>
        <state id="S2">State after detecting '10'.</state>
        <state id="S3">State after detecting '100'.</state>
        <state id="S4">State after detecting '1001'.</state>
        <state id="S5">State after detecting '10011'.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="current_state" width_description="[2:0]">Register to store the current state.</reg>
        <reg id="next_state" width_description="[2:0]">Register to store the next state.</reg>
        <always @(posedge CLK or posedge RST)>
            if (RST) begin
                current_state <= S0;
            end else begin
                current_state <= next_state;
            end
        </always>
    </logic_description>
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        <assign id="MATCH">Output signal indicating a match condition based on the FSM state.</assign>
        <always @(*) begin
            case(current_state)
                S0: begin
                    if(IN == 1'b1) begin
                        next_state = S1;
                        MATCH = 1'b0;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = S0;
                        MATCH = 1'b0;
                    end
                end
                S1: begin
                    if(IN == 1'b0) begin
                        next_state = S2;
                        MATCH = 1'b0;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = S1;
                        MATCH = 1'b0;
                    end
                end
                S2: begin
                    if(IN == 1'b0) begin
                        next_state = S3;
                        MATCH = 1'b0;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = S1;
                        MATCH = 1'b0;
                    end
                end
                S3: begin
                    if(IN == 1'b1) begin
                        next_state = S4;
                        MATCH = 1'b0;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = S0;
                        MATCH = 1'b0;
                    end
                end
                S4: begin
                    if(IN == 1'b1) begin
                        next_state = S5;
                        MATCH = 1'b1;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = S0;
                        MATCH = 1'b0;
                    end
                end
                S5: begin
                    if(IN == 1'b0) begin
                        next_state = S2;
                        MATCH = 1'b0;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = S1;
                        MATCH = 1'b0;
                    end
                end
            endcase
        end
    </logic_description>
</module>

<module id="pulse_detect">
    <description>Pulse detection logic based on input signal transitions.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="data_in">One-bit input signal.</input>
        <output id="data_out">Output signal indicating the presence of pulses.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="IDLE">Initial state.</state>
        <state id="PULSE_START">State indicating the start of a pulse.</state>
        <state id="PULSE_MIDDLE">State indicating the middle of a pulse.</state>
        <state id="PULSE_END">State indicating the end of a pulse.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="state" type="internal_signal">Register to store the current state.</reg>
        <reg id="next_state" type="internal_signal">Register to store the next state.</reg>
        
        <always_comb>
            case(state)
                IDLE: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b1)
                        next_state = PULSE_START;
                    else
                        next_state = IDLE;
                end
                
                PULSE_START: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b0)
                        next_state = PULSE_MIDDLE;
                    else
                        next_state = PULSE_START;
                end
                
                PULSE_MIDDLE: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b1)
                        next_state = PULSE_END;
                    else
                        next_state = IDLE;
                end
                
                PULSE_END: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b0)
                        next_state = IDLE;
                    else
                        next_state = PULSE_START;
                end
            endcase
        </always_comb>
        
        <always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n)> // Sequential logic block
            if(~rst_n)
                state <= IDLE;
            else
                state <= next_state;
        </always_ff>
    </logic_description>
    
    <implementation>
        Always block triggered on the positive edge of clk or the negative edge of rst_n.
        Inside the always block, use a case statement to handle different states.
        Depending on the current state, update the next_state variable based on the conditions specified in the if statements.
        In the sequential logic block, update the state register with the next_state value on the positive edge of clk or the negative edge of rst_n.
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="multi_pipe_4bit">
    <description>4-bit unsigned number pipeline multiplier with two levels of registers.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for chip reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="mul_a" width_description="[3:0]">Input signal representing the multiplicand with a data width of "size" bits.</input>
        <input id="mul_b" width_description="[3:0]">Input signal representing the multiplier with a data width of "size" bits.</input>
        <output id="mul_out" width_description="[7:0]">Product output signal with a data width of 2*size bits.</output>
    </ports>
    
    <parameter_description>
        <constant id="size" value="4">Size parameter for the multiplier.</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    
    <implementation>
        Extending input signals by adding 'size' number of zero bits at the most significant bit positions.
        
        Multiplication operation:
            Using a generate block to perform multiplication for each bit position of the multiplier (mul_b) and generate the partial products.
            For each bit position i from 0 to size-1, the partial product is calculated based on the corresponding bit in the multiplier.
            If the corresponding bit in the multiplier is 1, the multiplicand is left-shifted by i positions.
            If the corresponding bit in the multiplier is 0, the partial product is set to 0 ('d0).
            
        Add of partial products:
            Using registers to store the intermediate sum values.
            On the positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the module performs add operations.
            If the reset signal (rst_n) is low, indicating a reset condition, the registers are set to 0.
            If the reset signal (rst_n) is high, the registers are updated with the sum of the corresponding partial products.
            
        Final product calculation:
            On the positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the module calculates the final product.
            If the reset signal (rst_n) is low, indicating a reset condition, the product output (mul_out) is set to 0.
            If the reset signal (rst_n) is high, the product output (mul_out) is updated with the sum of registers.
    </implementation>
    
</module>

samples.xml_description:
<module id="alu">
    <description>ALU for a 32-bit MIPS-ISA CPU. Supports various arithmetic and logical operations.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="a" width_description="[31:0]">A 32-bit input operand.</input>
        <input id="b" width_description="[31:0]">A 32-bit input operand.</input>
        <input id="aluc" width_description="[5:0]">Control signal for selecting the operation to be performed.</input>
        <output id="r" width_description="[31:0]">Result of the operation.</output>
        <output id="zero">Indicates if the result is zero.</output>
        <output id="carry">Indicates if a carry occurred during the operation.</output>
        <output id="negative">Indicates if the result is negative.</output>
        <output id="overflow">Indicates if an overflow occurred during the operation.</output>
        <output id="flag">General flag output, set based on specific operations (SLT and SLTU).</output>
    </ports>
    
    <parameter_description>
        <param id="ADD">6'b100000</param>
        <param id="ADDU">6'b100001</param>
        <param id="SUB">6'b100010</param>
        <param id="SUBU">6'b100011</param>
        <param id="AND">6'b100100</param>
        <param id="OR">6'b100101</param>
        <param id="XOR">6'b100110</param>
        <param id="NOR">6'b100111</param>
        <param id="SLT">6'b101010</param>
        <param id="SLTU">6'b101011</param>
        <param id="SLL">6'b000000</param>
        <param id="SRL">6'b000010</param>
        <param id="SRA">6'b000011</param>
        <param id="SLLV">6'b000100</param>
        <param id="SRLV">6'b000110</param>
        <param id="SRAV">6'b000111</param>
        <param id="LUI">6'b001111</param>
    </parameter_description>
    
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        <assign id="signed_a">($signed(a))</assign>
        <assign id="signed_b">($signed(b))</assign>
        <assign id="res[31:0]">(r)</assign>
        
        <assign id="flag">((aluc == SLT || aluc == SLTU) ? 1'b1 : 1'bz)</assign>
        <assign id="zero">({{31{1'b0}}, res[31]} == 1'b1 ? 1'b1 : 1'b0)</assign>
    </logic_description>
    
    <implementation>
        reg [32:0] res;
        assign r = res[31:0];
        assign flag = ((aluc == SLT || aluc == SLTU) ? 1'b1 : 1'bz);
        assign zero = ({31'd0, res[31]} == 1'b1 ? 1'b1 : 1'b0);
        always @(*) begin
            case(aluc)
                ADD: res <= signed_a + signed_b;
                ADDU: res <= a + b;
                SUB: res <= signed_a - signed_b;
                SUBU: res <= a - b;
                AND: res <= a & b;
                OR: res <= a | b;
                XOR: res <= a ^ b;
                NOR: res <= ~(a | b);
                SLT: res <= {32'd0, signed_a < signed_b};
                SLTU: res <= {32'd0, a < b};
                SLL: res <= b << a[4:0];
                SRL: res <= b >> a[4:0];
                SRA: res <= signed_b >>> signed_a[4:0];
                SLLV: res <= b << a[4:0];
                SRLV: res <= b >> a[4:0];
                SRAV: res <= signed_b >>> signed_a[4:0];
                LUI: res <= {a[15:0], 16'd0};
                default: res <= 32'dz;
            endcase
        end
    </implementation>
endmodule
